name: Ollama
notes: Primary local AI runtime. Keep local-only unless protected by firewall/auth.

name: llama.cpp
notes: Core local inference engine for GGUF models.

name: whisper.cpp
notes: Offline speech-to-text foundation.

name: KittenTTS
notes: Small TTS candidate; verify license and maintenance before shipping.

name: Continue.dev
notes: AI coding workflow for VS Code/local models.

name: Aider
notes: Git-aware coding assistant.

name: skills.sh
notes: Reusable AI skill ecosystem; verify per-skill licenses.

name: Smart Ralph
notes: Spec-driven coding-agent workflow reference.

name: Graphify
notes: Candidate for code/document knowledge graphs.

name: Agent Memory Techniques
notes: Reference collection for conversation, episodic, semantic, working, vector, graph, and production memory patterns. Verify licenses for linked subprojects separately.

name: Graphiti
notes: Knowledge-graph memory candidate mentioned through agent memory research. Needs direct repo/license verification.

name: Mem0
notes: Long-term memory candidate; verify exact repo, license, and deployment model before adoption.

name: olmOCR
notes: OCR/document ingestion for PDFs and scans.

name: TheAlgorithms Python
notes: Algorithm and data structure reference.

name: Wikipedia Dumps
notes: Useful offline encyclopedia; license requires attribution and share-alike handling.

name: Stack Exchange Data Dump
notes: Programming Q&A archive; track attribution requirements.

name: Awesome Public Datasets
notes: Catalog of public datasets. Each dataset needs separate license and privacy review before commercial use.

name: 21st.dev
notes: UI component inspiration; check each component license before use.

name: Logto
notes: Open-source auth platform; evaluate if VoltanLabs needs accounts.

name: Uptime Kuma
notes: Self-hosted uptime monitoring for VoltanLabs, AI services, and future backend endpoints.

name: File Browser
notes: Self-hosted file manager for local archives, sprite assets, datasets, and documentation. Secure with strong auth before network exposure.

name: Neon
notes: Serverless Postgres platform. Useful later, but not needed for local-first DataByteSprites right now.

name: SurrealDB
notes: Powerful database, but SQLite remains first choice for now.

name: SQLite
notes: First-choice local database for browser/local-first systems. Use before heavier database platforms unless a real backend need appears.

name: DuckDB
notes: SQLite-like embedded analytics database. Strong candidate for local dataset exploration and vault reporting.

name: TurboVec
notes: High-performance vector search candidate. Benchmark against FAISS/Chroma before adoption.

name: FAISS
notes: Foundational vector similarity search library. Benchmark against TurboVec and Chroma for our local AI archive.

name: ChromaDB
notes: Local-friendly vector database for embeddings, retrieval, and AI memory prototypes.

name: jcode Agent Harness
notes: Agent harness/orchestration candidate. Needs direct repo verification and security review before use.

name: Tree-sitter
notes: Parser generator used to understand code structure. Strong foundation for project search, documentation, and AI-assisted refactoring.

name: ripgrep
notes: Extremely fast code/text search. Core tool for local development and repo inspection.

name: JSON Schema Store
notes: Useful for validating structured game data, vault entries, configs, and AI-generated JSON assets.

name: DevDocs
notes: Offline-capable documentation browser. Good candidate for the local knowledge workstation.

name: MkDocs
notes: Simple Markdown documentation generator. Strong candidate for internal docs and manuals.

name: Docusaurus
notes: Powerful documentation site generator. Consider when the vault/wiki outgrows static pages.

name: HTMX
notes: Adds interactivity with less JavaScript. Good candidate for future simple app-like pages.

name: Alpine.js
notes: Tiny JavaScript framework for lightweight interactivity without a heavy frontend stack.

name: LDtk
notes: Modern 2D level editor. Strong candidate for future DataByteSprites world/map design.

name: Kenney Assets
notes: Excellent commercial-friendly placeholder assets. Verify each pack license before final shipping.

name: OpenGameArt
notes: Large asset archive. Every individual asset needs its own license check before use in commercial projects.

name: Aseprite File Readers
notes: Open-source libraries for reading .ase/.aseprite files could help build a free sprite workflow without relying on paid tools.

name: TruffleHog
notes: Powerful secret scanner. License requires careful review before embedding; safe as a standalone dev/security tool.

name: Gitleaks
notes: Secret scanner to prevent API keys, passwords, and tokens from entering VoltanLabs repositories.

name: Syft
notes: Creates software bills of materials so we can track dependencies and license obligations.

name: Grype
notes: Scans dependencies and container images for known vulnerabilities. Good addition to VoltanLabs Verified pipeline.

name: Penpot
notes: Open-source design/prototyping tool. Strong self-hostable alternative to proprietary design platforms.

name: Blockbench
notes: Great voxel and low-poly model editor. Keep for future creative experiments; review license obligations if integrating plugins/assets.

name: AI4AnimationPy
notes: Non-commercial license means do not ship in monetized products without permission.

name: Scrapling
notes: Use responsibly; respect robots, terms, and site policies.

name: GLM 5.2 Abliterated GGUF
notes: Do not integrate because it is marketed around removed safeguards. Evaluate models by license, safety, performance, and project fit instead.
